explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Policy documents reference practices with third parties for personalized content and advertising. These documents also reference a framework for data use with third parties.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This GPM Investments Privacy Policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports the privacy pillar by detailing how personal data is used for profiling and targeted advertising, and crucially, by offering users an explicit opt-out mechanism for such processing, thereby demonstrating user control over automated systems. The policy also touches upon governance by mentioning its practices with third parties for personalized content and advertising, implying a framework for data use.
relevant_sources.title: GPM Investments Privacy Policy

oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.findings: Policy documents describe how personal data is used for profiling and targeted advertising. They also state that an explicit opt-out mechanism is offered to users for such processing, describing user control over automated systems.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

It supports the privacy pillar by detailing how personal data is used for profiling and targeted advertising, and crucially, by offering users an explicit opt-out mechanism for such processing, thereby demonstrating user control over automated systems. transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: ARKO's policy documents describe how personal data is used for profiling and targeted advertising, and also state an explicit opt-out mechanism is offered to users for this processing. Additionally, ARKO's published materials reference practices with third parties for personalized content and advertising, and a framework for data use with third parties. These disclosures mean that oversight, privacy, and governance are addressed at the policy level, accounting for 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. This assessment draws on 2 publicly available sources. No qualifying public evidence was found for transparency, fairness, and 2 additional pillars.
